title: Notebook cells contain potential hard-coded secrets (1 cell(s) across 1 notebook(s))

evidence.note: Pattern-based scan — review each match before acting; false positives are possible.

recommendation: Replace literal keys / SAS / tokens / passwords with Key Vault lookups via notebookutils.credentials.getSecret(...) or a workspace-managed-identity flow.

title: Notebook cells install packages inline (%pip / !pip / %conda) (1 cell(s) across 1 notebook(s))

evidence.note: Pattern-based scan — review each match before acting; false positives are possible.

recommendation: Move dependencies to a Fabric environment so installs are baked in once, not repeated on every run, and so library versions are pinned across promotions.

title: Notebook cells call unbounded .collect() / .toPandas() on Spark DataFrames (1 cell(s) across 1 notebook(s))

evidence.note: Pattern-based scan — review each match before acting; false positives are possible.

recommendation: Bare .collect()/.toPandas() pulls every row to the driver and is a common OOM cause. Bound the result first (.limit(N), .first(), .head(N), .take(N), or aggregate to a single row), or persist with .write.format("delta") instead of materialising on the driver. Note: Fabric's display() is safe — it auto-truncates and is not flagged by this rule.

title: Notebook cells use Databricks-only APIs (1 cell(s) across 1 notebook(s))

evidence.note: Pattern-based scan — review each match before acting; false positives are possible.

recommendation: Replace dbutils.* with notebookutils.*, /dbfs paths with abfss:// or attached lakehouse references, and Databricks MLflow URIs with the Fabric MLflow integration.

title: Notebook cells embed hard-coded abfss:// paths or workspace / lakehouse GUIDs (1 cell(s) across 1 notebook(s))

evidence.note: Pattern-based scan — review each match before acting; false positives are possible.

recommendation: Parameterise the lakehouse / path, or rely on the notebook's attached default lakehouse. Hard-coded GUIDs break dev->test->prod promotion via deployment pipelines and Git integration.

title: Notebook cells write data in non-Delta formats (parquet / csv / json / orc / avro) (1 cell(s) across 1 notebook(s))

evidence.note: Pattern-based scan — review each match before acting; false positives are possible.

recommendation: Prefer .format("delta"). Delta gives ACID, time travel, V-Order and is required for Direct Lake. Non-Delta tables silently lose those capabilities in OneLake.
